data.title: MP Materials started Buy at Goldman; strategic alignment with U.S. policy unlocks significant value
data.description:
MP Materials (MP) +8.9% in Wednesday's trading as Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $77 price target, noting the company is set to become the largest vertically integrated rare earth magnet supplier in North America, uniquely positioned to capture market share from China-based producers and refiners as domestic manufacturers look to diversify supply chains.

MP's (MP) primary focus is on NdPr oxide, a key component for making permanent magnets used in electronics, electric vehicles and defense systems, and critical to key components of U.S. manufacturing, and Goldman's Brian Lee believes the company's downstream expansion into refining and magnet production, accelerated by a partnership with the U.S. government, will strategically position MP as a key supply chain component for rare earth refining and magnet production, of which China-based players currently control 90%-95%; as a result, Lee said MP should enjoy significant revenue and EBITDA growth, while vertical integration unlocks margin upside.

MP Materials (MP) also trades higher on the day's earlier announcement that it established a joint venture in Saudi Arabia with the U.S. Department of War and Saudi Arabia's state-owned Maaden to develop a rare earth refinery in the kingdom.

data.title: Jack Dorsey maps out Block's automation push during investor day (update)
data.description:
Block (XYZ) Co-Founder, Head and Chairman Jack Dorsey kicked off his company's 2025 investor day Wednesday highlighting its focus on becoming a "fully autonomous economic platform for both individuals and businesses."

Sending its stock some 7% higher in midafternoon trading, the company introduced strong gross profit guidance for full-year 2026, calling for 17% Y/Y growth to $11.98B (vs. $11.86B Visible Alpha consensus. It also expects FY26 adjusted EPS to advance over 30% Y/Y to $3.20, trailing the $3.31 average analyst estimate.

For the next three years, it expects gross profit rising in the med-teens range annually through 2028, reaching about $15.8B, and adjusted EPS growing in the low 30% range annually through 2028 to $5.50. Rule of 40 is expected to be achieved next year and sustain through 2028.

Shortly before the fintech unveiled its guidance, Dorsey outlined a "master plan" on how Block (XYZ) will "build into our brand the right technology, so that we are future-proof, so that we have an interface that lasts a decade, and so we can be truly proactive and get more and more customers to consider us and also to stay with us."

Part of that plan includes creating the so-called "managerbot," which Dorsey described as a way for a business to automate payrolls, scheduling, inventory and marketing, effectively reducing manual work. "You can truly see it as running your business," he said. In addition, its consumer-focused "moneybot" was designed to automate budgeting, taxes, savings and payments.

These two tools, among others, are set to "eventually take over the interface" of Block's (XYZ) Square and Cash App ecosystems, Dorsey said.

On bitcoin (BTC-USD), Dorsey said the crypto represents an alternative, global payments option, and "more and more merchants will be driving their customers to lower cost transactions, such as bitcoin or stablecoins."

data.title: A Japanese-style pop-up arcade concept in LA could roll out nationally if successful
data.description:
A new concept store in Los Angeles called Gacha & Catch will be open for six months as a test for the public's appetite for Japanese anime and manga culture, according to Nikkei Asia. The Gacha and Catch store will be an immersive, interactive space where visitors can try their luck with over 100 Gacha capsule toy machines (dispensers that provide collectible toys in plastic capsules) and claw machines. The space aims to recreate the fun and thrill of Japanese arcades.

Japanese conglomerate Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF), Sega Sammy (OTCPK:SGAMY) (OTCPK:SGAMF), and toy maker Tomy (OTCPK:TOMYY) (OTCPK:TOMYF) are partnering on the new concept store.  If the test proves successful, the partners plan to expand the format, potentially opening several hundred U.S. locations starting in fiscal 2026.

Sega (OTCPK:SGAMY) (SMAMF), known for video games and crane game expertise, has been building out its physical retail presence with new stores in Shanghai and Tokyo, as well as a pop-up shop in Los Angeles earlier this year. Its iconic Sonic the Hedgehog character has strong popularity in the U.S., making the market particularly attractive. Meanwhile, Tomy (OTCPK:TOMYY) (OTCPK:TOMYF), famous for Licca-chan dolls and Beyblade toys, will add its strength in capsule toy machines. The company is already collaborating with Genda to roll out similar machines in North American malls and theaters.

If Gacha and Catch rolled out nationally, Dave & Buster's (PLAY) would be the most notable chain at risk of losing incremental sales.

On a broad level, Japanese companies view the U.S. as a major opportunity, with a content market valued at 84 trillion yen ($536 billion).

data.title: Wix anticipates BASE44 to reach $50M ARR by year-end 2025 as AI-powered app building accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Wix.com Ltd. (WIX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Avishai Abrahami introduced the transformative potential of "vibe coding," emphasizing its role in democratizing software creation for non-developers and likening it to Wix's original mission for website building. He highlighted, "vibe coding is unlocking access for regular people to build software intuitively without any technical barriers."
  • Abrahami reported significant traction in the AI-powered app building space, stating, "BASE44's share of audience traffic increased from almost nothing to more than 10% in October... BASE44 is quickly proving to be a leader and the best solution on the market today." He expressed anticipation for a new flagship product expected in early 2026.
  • President Nir Zohar discussed continued robust new user cohort behavior and the success of BASE44, noting, "Returns on our initial marketing investments meaningfully exceeded expectations as demand ramped through the quarter. As a result, we were able to confidently scale marketing efforts above our initial August plan." BASE44 now serves over 2 million users, with more than 1,000 new paying subscribers joining daily.
  • CFO Lior Shemesh stated, "Total bookings grew to $515 million in quarter 3, up 14% year-over-year... Total revenue grew to $505 million, also up 14% year-over-year and above the high end of our guidance range." Shemesh highlighted the impact of BASE44's different subscription model, noting short-term headwinds to free cash flow and operating profit due to upfront costs, but anticipated margin improvements as the business scales. "We now anticipate BASE44 to achieve at least $50 million of ARR by year-end, an increase from our previous expectations."

Outlook

  • Management raised its full-year bookings outlook to a range of $2,060 million to $2,078 million, or 13% to 14% year-over-year growth, up from the prior 11% to 13% range, citing "meaningful outperformance of BASE44."
  • The full-year revenue outlook was updated to $1,990 million to $2,000 million, up 13% to 14% year-over-year, shifting toward the high end of the prior 12% to 14% range.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin is now expected at 68% to 69% of revenue for the full year, and free cash flow is projected at approximately $600 million, or 30% of revenue.
  • BASE44 is anticipated to reach at least $50 million in ARR by year-end 2025, up from earlier guidance.

Financial Results

  • Total bookings reached $515 million in Q3, marking a 14% year-over-year increase. Total revenue was $505 million, also up 14% year-over-year.
  • Partners revenue rose to $192 million, a 24% increase year-over-year, driven by designer and studio adoption.
  • Transaction revenue was $65 million, up 20% year-over-year, with GPV at $3.7 billion, a 13% increase.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin was 69%, slightly down from 70% in Q2, reflecting BASE44's early-stage costs. Non-GAAP operating income was $90 million or 18% of revenue. Free cash flow stood at $159 million, or 32% of revenue.
  • The company repurchased approximately 1.3 million shares for $175 million, with $225 million remaining in the buyback program.

Q&A

  • Ygal Arounian, Citigroup: Asked about monthly versus annual subscriptions for BASE44 and churn expectations. CEO Abrahami acknowledged, "at this stage, lean a lot more towards a monthly subscription than annual subscription... churn is higher than the standard Wix... But base is better than we expected and we know there's so much more we can do."
  • Arounian followed up on costs and gross margins. Abrahami responded, "there's a lot we can do on cost... But I think that long term... we can dramatically improve the cost of AI for BASE44." CFO Shemesh added, "new users coming to BASE, they are obviously consuming more AI tokens... the profit, obviously, will be totally different" in future periods as cohorts mature.
  • Deepak Mathivanan, Cantor Fitzgerald: Inquired about Wix's strategy for making websites agent-ready for AI discovery. Abrahami explained Wix websites are now indexable by LLMs and emphasized ongoing adaptation to new AI-driven standards.
  • Mathivanan also asked about BASE44 cohort retention. President Zohar said retention is "better than what you used to see at Wix in the early days" and expects improvement as brand visibility grows.
  • Andrew Boone, Citizens JMP: Asked about scaling margins for BASE44. CFO Shemesh explained margin pressure is short-term due to high demand and upfront costs, but expects profitability to align with core Wix as the business matures.
  • Josh Beck, Raymond James: Asked about margin drag from BASE44's growth. Shemesh stated, "this kind of drag is a drag that we really like... I believe that the margin just will continue to improve as we already started to see that from Q3 to Q4."
  • Trevor Young, Barclays: Queried about delays in the new self-creator tool. Abrahami attributed delays to technical challenges, but said the product is "entering already the first stage of a closed beta within here inside of Wix."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, pressing for details on BASE44's subscription dynamics, cost management, and margin trajectory, but acknowledged growth and strategic positioning.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing strong demand, expectations for margin improvement, and strategic product launches. Abrahami and Shemesh consistently expressed optimism and long-term confidence, with phrases like "we are very optimistic" and "I expect BASE44 to have similar operating and free cash flow margins to Wix."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted further toward enthusiasm about BASE44's growth and its impact on guidance, while analysts maintained a consistent level of scrutiny regarding costs and execution risk.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw an increased focus on BASE44's rapid growth, with management raising full-year bookings and revenue guidance, attributing the majority of the increase to BASE44's outperformance, unlike the previous quarter where core Wix cohorts were the main driver.
  • Margin pressure due to BASE44's upfront costs was acknowledged as a positive sign of growth, whereas last quarter's narrative emphasized operating leverage and stable free cash flow margin.
  • Strategic focus has shifted more explicitly toward AI-powered app building and capturing market share in the vibe coding space.
  • Analysts remained focused on subscription mix, churn, and cost structure, but there was heightened interest in BASE44's integration and margin evolution.
  • Management's confidence in long-term profitability and product innovation has strengthened compared to the previous call.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited short-term headwinds to operating profit and free cash flow from BASE44's monthly subscription mix and associated upfront AI compute and marketing costs.
  • Churn for BASE44 is currently higher than traditional Wix but management believes it will improve as the platform matures.
  • There are technical challenges and delays in launching the new self-creator tool, though management expects resolution in early 2026.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about margin compression and cost discipline, seeking clarity on when margin expansion might resume.

Final Takeaway

Wix management underscored the transformative opportunity in AI-powered app building, with BASE44's rapid growth driving a revision of full-year guidance and setting expectations for at least $50 million in ARR by year-end 2025. While short-term margin pressure is expected due to upfront investments, management projects that as the BASE44 user base matures and cost efficiencies are realized, profitability will align with core Wix levels. The company remains confident in its ability to capture market share, innovate, and deliver long-term value through both its traditional website platform and emerging AI-driven offerings.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ramaco Resources started at Sell by Goldman Sachs on Brook mine skepticism
data.description:
Ramaco Resources (METC) -11.1% in Wednesday's trading as Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Sell rating and $16 price target, saying the company still needs to meaningfully de-risk its Brook Mine asset to warrant its current valuation.

Goldman's Brian Lee believes Ramaco's (METC) Brook Mine will be more expensive from an operating and capital standpoint than the company currently forecasts and sees insufficient demand for scandium to support the large volumes the company aims to produce.

Ramaco (METC) has been focused mostly on the production of met coal but its stock has dislocated from historical valuations due to a greater focus on its rare earth deposit, but Lee noted that the grades of the Brook Mine deposit are much lower than traditional assets; as a result, the analyst thinks further derisking is required to increase confidence in the commercial viability of the asset and sees meaningful risks of developing the Brook Mine asset.

While the Brook Mine aims to produce several minerals, scandium is expected to support 59% of the revenue despite only accounting for 5% of overall production, and the scandium market is already oversupplied, which has caused pricing to meaningfully decline over recent years, and the future demand outlook remains mostly limited to solid oxide fuel cells, Lee said.

The analyst also pointed to deteriorating market dynamics for met coal, driven by shifting supply-demand dynamics and heightened volatility, with market prices continuing to decline since the Q1 2022 peak, and he expects continued near-term weakness before seeing a gradual
improvement through 2028.

data.title: Trump jokes about firing Bessent if rates not lowered
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump, growing impatient with the Federal Reserve's pace of interest-rate cuts, quipped that he would oust Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent if he fails to help deliver lower interest rates.

“The only thing Scott’s blowing it on is the Fed,” Trump said at a U.S.-Saudi investment event in Washington. He added in a joking tone: “rates are too high, Scott, and if you don’t get it fixed fast, I’m going to fire" you. "Okay?”

Trump repeated that he would like to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell. On Tuesday, Trump said he has started interviewing candidates for the next Fed chief and hinted he has already settled on his choice. Powell's term ends in May 2026.

As Treasury head, Bessent has no authority to set rates. That responsibility lies with the central bank's Federal Open Market Committee. The FOMC has cut rates twice this year, though Trump has been pushing for far deeper and faster reductions. Trump has said he wants Bessent to lead the monetary authority, but Bessent doesn't want to leave his Treasury post.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Axalta shares seen rangebound as RBC downgrades stock after $25B Akzo Nobel merger deal
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets on Wednesday downgraded Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) to Sector Perform from a previous investment rating of Outperform, saying the company’s newly announced $25 billion all-stock merger with Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) (OTCQX:AKZOY) offers limited near-term upside for shareholders.

The merger of equals, unveiled November 18, will create a coatings giant with roughly $17 billion in pro-forma revenue and $3.3 billion in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, supported by an estimated $600 million in cost synergies. Axalta (AXTA) investors will receive 0.6539 Akzo shares for each Axalta share, implying a takeout value of about $30.58, only a roughly 9% premium to Axalta’s pre-announcement share price, according to RBC.

RBC analysts led by Arun Viswanathan said the small premium, combined with muted growth trends across key coatings markets, tempers expectations for a meaningful rerating. The firm noted that most of the merger’s benefits, including synergy capture, tax advantages and potential index inclusion tied to a future NYSE-only listing, will take up to five years to fully materialize.

The combined company plans to operate across seven segments, including Decorative, Refinish, Industrial and Aerospace, and will target net leverage of 2.0 to 2.5 times. Both firms will suspend share repurchases until the transaction closes, expected in late 2026 or early 2027.

RBC cut its price target on Axalta (AXTA) to $30 from $35, citing slower structural growth in refinish and industrial coatings, a lack of competing bidders, and the long timeline before value is realized.

data.title: Exact Sciences likely needs to see over $100/share in takeover - analyst
data.description:
Exact Sciences (EXAS) will likely need to see over $100 a share in a takeover, according to  a William Blair analyst, after an earlier report that Abbott Labs (ABT) is talks to buy the cancer test maker.

Shares of Exact Sciences (EXAS) soared 21% to ~$84/share after Bloomberg reported the takeover discussions with a transaction possible within the coming days. Abbott Labs fell 3.6%.

"From a valuation perspective, we believe a takeout multiple between 6 and 7 times forward sales would be required," William Blair analyst Andrew Brackmann wrote in a note on Wednesday. "This would be a 35%-plus premium to the current multiple of just under 5 times, a roughly 20% premium to the five-year average of roughly 5.6 times, and in line with smaller but similarly growing profitable labs that trade in this range."

Exact Sciences (EXAS) is known for its Cologuard colon cancer test. 

"Overall, with limited details, EXAS would offer a solid brand and strong beachhead into the broader oncology testing market across screening," Barclays analyst Luke Sergott wrote in a note on Wednesday.

data.title: Bitcoin resumes slide after brief bounce, falling back to below $90K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) resumed its price decline Wednesday as the crypto struggled to hold the key $90K technical support level.

The highest-profile token retreated 4.5% in the last 24 hours to $88.8K as of 2:51 p.m. ET, reversing Tuesday's bounce to as high as $93.7K. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was off 30% from its all-time high of over $126K at press time.

"Bitcoin continues to work through a challenging market phase defined by weakening market structure, retreating speculative demand, and a decisive shift toward risk-off positioning across derivatives," Glassnode said in a report. "Spot-based demand remains absent, ETF flows are negative, and futures markets show no appetite to add leverage into weakness. Meanwhile, implied volatility, skew, and hedging flows all point toward heightened concern for near-term downside risk, with investors paying increasingly high premiums for protection."

Much of the cryptoverse was under pressure intraday. Ether (ETH-USD) dropped 7.9% to $2.87K, Monero (XMR-USD) -11%, Cronos (CRO-USD) -7.7%, Sui (SUI-USD) -9.6%, Uniswap (UNI-USD) -8.8%, Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -8.6%, XRP (XRP-USD) -8.2% and Solana (SOL-USD) -6.9%.

Crypto-linked stocks were also trading in the red as major equity indices looked for direction. Strategy (MSTR), which recently disclosed it bought the BTC dip, tumbled 11%, Coinbase Global (COIN) -5%, Gemini Space Station (GEMI) -8.2%, Bullish (BLSH) -6.3%, Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) -7.6%, MARA Holdings (MARA) -8.2% and Circle Internet Group (CRCL) -11%.

The path forward for bitcoin (BTC-USD) "depends on whether demand can re-emerge around key cost-basis levels or whether current fragility gives way to a deeper corrective phase or bear market," as the market searches for stability, Glassnode added.

data.title: Nomura turns bullish on Sony citing improved growth prospects for entertainment unit
data.description:
• Nomura on Wednesday upgraded Sony (SONY) to "buy" from a previous investment rating of "neutral."
  • The research firm sees improved growth prospects for Sony's entertainment unit while continuing to expect consistently strong profits at its game & network services and music segments.
  • "The company has been working to expand subscription streaming businesses for some time, and we raise our rating to Buy because we think profit growth prospects have improved as these efforts start to bear fruit," Nomura said in its research note.
  • The research firm said it expects profit contributions to be driven by, among other things, PlayStation Plus subscriber growth, faster growth in music streaming, and improvements in the product mix for image sensors.
  • For the music segment, they said Sony will benefit from the renewal of contracts with digital service providers.
  • For the networks segment, Nomura noted Sony's y-o-y growth in subscriber numbers and a shift towards high-priced services.
  • "With rival Xbox Game Pass scheduled to hike prices sharply in November 2025, we expect PS Plus membership to remain on an upward trajectory and margins to remain high," Nomura said.
